member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.DefaultFrameClock
member.summary: Production default for — uses for monotonic time.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.DefaultEntropySource
member.summary: Production default for — uses .

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.SingleThreadTileScheduler
member.summary: Production default for — sequential, single-threaded execution.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.EmbeddedShaderSource
member.summary: Production default for — looks up embedded resources.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.PhysicalFileSystem
member.summary: Production default for — maps directly to .

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IEntropySource
member.summary: Provides cryptographically-safe entropy for deterministic rendering. All non-deterministic RNG must route through this seam.
member.remarks: Failure to use this seam causes pixel-identical output guarantees to be violated, as RNG state varies across machines and runs.

member.name: M:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IEntropySource.Fill(System.Span{System.Byte})
member.summary: Fills the specified span with entropy bytes.

member.name: M:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IEntropySource.NextUInt64
member.summary: Returns the next 64-bit unsigned integer.

member.name: M:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IEntropySource.NextUInt32
member.summary: Returns the next 32-bit unsigned integer.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IFileSystem
member.summary: Provides file system access for deterministic rendering. All file I/O must route through this seam.
member.remarks: Failure to use this seam causes pixel-identical output guarantees to be violated, as file system state varies across machines and runs. The production default maps straight to ; test doubles use in-memory storage.

member.name: M:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IFileSystem.ReadAllBytes(System.String)
member.summary: Reads the contents of the file at as bytes.

member.name: M:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IFileSystem.ReadAllText(System.String)
member.summary: Reads the contents of the file at as text.

member.name: M:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IFileSystem.WriteAllBytes(System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Writes to the file at .

member.name: M:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IFileSystem.WriteAllText(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Writes to the file at .

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IFrameClock
member.summary: Provides monotonic time and frame sequencing for deterministic rendering. All non-deterministic wall-clock access must route through this seam.
member.remarks: Failure to use this seam causes pixel-identical output guarantees to be violated, as wall-clock time varies across machines and runs.

member.name: M:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IFrameClock.NowNanos
member.summary: Returns monotonic time in nanoseconds.

member.name: P:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IFrameClock.FrameCounter
member.summary: Returns the current frame counter. Implementations must guarantee FrameCounter increases by exactly 1 per frame.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IShaderSource
member.summary: Provides shader source code for deterministic rendering. All shader lookups must route through this seam.

member.name: M:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IShaderSource.GetSpirv(Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.ShaderId)
member.summary: Returns the SPIR-V bytecode for the specified shader.

member.name: M:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.IShaderSource.GetWgsl(Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.ShaderId)
member.summary: Returns the WGSL source for the specified shader.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.ShaderId
member.summary: Identifies a shader for lookup via .

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.TileBody`1
member.summary: Delegate for tile body iteration.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.ITileScheduler
member.summary: Schedules tile processing for deterministic parallel execution. All non-deterministic task scheduling must route through this seam.
member.remarks: Failure to use this seam causes pixel-identical output guarantees to be violated, as thread ordering varies across machines and runs. Implementations may be sequential or deterministic-parallel, but must guarantee ordered reduction per D-014.

member.name: M:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.ITileScheduler.ForEachTile``1(System.Int32,``0,Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.TileBody{``0})
member.summary: Iterates over each tile, invoking with the tile index and state. Implementations choose sequential or deterministic-parallel execution.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.Testing.FixedFrameClock
member.summary: Test double for — fixed time and frame counter for deterministic replay.
member.remarks: This type is compiled into the main assembly but is for test use only.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.Testing.DeterministicEntropySource
member.summary: Test double for — deterministic Xorshift64 for reproducible output.
member.remarks: This type is compiled into the main assembly but is for test use only.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.Testing.DeterministicParallelTileScheduler
member.summary: Test double for — deterministic parallel execution with ordered reduction.
member.remarks: This type is compiled into the main assembly but is for test use only. Results are invariant under thread count per D-014 parallel reduction contract.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.Testing.InMemoryShaderSource
member.summary: Test double for — in-memory shader storage for deterministic testing.
member.remarks: This type is compiled into the main assembly but is for test use only.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.Determinism.Testing.InMemoryFileSystem
member.summary: Test double for — in-memory file storage for deterministic testing.
member.remarks: This type is compiled into the main assembly but is for test use only.

member.name: T:Etch.Abstractions.EtchExtensionPointAttribute
member.summary: Marks an interface as an intentional extension point, exempting it from the ET0107 (no-single-implementer) rule.

member.name: T:Etch.EtchException
member.summary: The only exception type thrown by Etch. Carries a stable and an optional so logs and crash dumps can correlate an incident to the exact source location that raised it.

remarks.para: Shape contract. Sealed; no serialization constructor; no reflection-dependent behavior; a single inner-exception link at most (panic codes carry context; stacks do the rest). The type is AOT-clean and safe to throw from trimmed / Native AOT builds.

When to throw directly. Almost never. Prefer and its siblings — they are [DoesNotReturn], capture caller file and line, and keep the throw shape consistent. ET0108 (NoRawThrowAnalyzer) forbids raw throw new EtchException(...) outside the Panic helpers themselves.

member.name: P:Etch.EtchException.Code
member.summary: The stable ET-P-#### identifier for the panic class.

member.name: P:Etch.EtchException.CallSite
member.summary: The source file and line where the panic originated, formatted as <path>:<line>. when the call site was not captured (e.g. exception constructed manually by a non- caller).

member.name: M:Etch.EtchException.#ctor(Etch.PanicCode,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an with the given code, message, and optional call site.

member.name: M:Etch.EtchException.#ctor(Etch.PanicCode,System.String,System.Exception,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an wrapping a single inner exception. Use only at the boundary where native or third-party code has already produced the inner exception — internal Etch code does not chain.

member.name: T:Etch.Panic
member.summary: Static entry points that throw with a captured call site. Every internal Etch failure path funnels through one of these methods so that logs, crash dumps, and the verifier see a consistent shape.

remarks.para: All methods are -annotated. The C# compiler uses this to treat code after a Panic.* call as unreachable, so a method that ends with Panic.Invariant(...) and no return statement still type-checks.
The file and line parameters are populated by the compiler via and ; callers must not pass them explicitly.

member.name: M:Etch.Panic.Invariant(Etch.PanicCode,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Raises a generic invariant violation with the supplied and . Captures the caller's file path and line number into .

member.name: M:Etch.Panic.ArgumentNull(System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Raises naming .

member.name: M:Etch.Panic.ArgumentOutOfRange(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Raises naming and optionally including an extra explaining the valid range.

member.name: M:Etch.Panic.NotImplemented(System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Raises naming the that was invoked but is not wired up on this build or platform.

member.name: T:Etch.PanicCode
member.summary: Stable identifier for a panic or exception condition. Backed by a string of the form ET-P-#### where #### is a zero-padded sequential number. Codes are the primary key shared by logs, crash dumps, the scene-reproducer verifier, and support diagnostics, so they must remain stable once allocated.
member.remarks: is a thin wrapper over rather than an enum so that the registry in can grow without recompiling every downstream assembly, and so that codes survive round-trips through text logs and structured telemetry without losing their exact spelling.

member.name: P:Etch.PanicCode.Value
member.summary: The ET-P-#### identifier.

member.name: M:Etch.PanicCode.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Wraps the specified code string. Callers should use the constants on rather than constructing codes ad-hoc.

member.name: T:Etch.PanicCodes
member.summary: Central registry of allocated panic codes. Every code used anywhere in Etch must appear here; the living catalogue of meanings lives in docs/00-overview/panic-codes.md and is the single source of truth for human-readable context.
member.remarks: When adding a code: append a constant here, append a row to panic-codes.md, and leave the numeric suffix monotonically increasing. Never re-use a retired number.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.BufferOverflow
member.summary: ET-P-0001 — write past the end of a fixed-size buffer.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.UnexpectedAllocation
member.summary: ET-P-0002 — managed allocation detected inside an AllocGuard scope.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.InvariantViolation
member.summary: ET-P-0003 — generic invariant violation with caller-supplied message.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.ArgumentNull
member.summary: ET-P-0004 — a non-null parameter contract received .

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.ArgumentOutOfRange
member.summary: ET-P-0005 — an argument fell outside its declared range.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.InvalidState
member.summary: ET-P-0006 — operation invoked on a type in a state that forbids it (post-dispose, pre-init, etc.).

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.NotImplemented
member.summary: ET-P-0007 — a declared feature path is not implemented on this build/platform.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.SceneReproVersionMismatch
member.summary: ET-P-0020 — scene-reproducer file version is newer than supported by the reader.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.GpuValidation
member.summary: ET-P-0200 — wgpu validation error (validation layer caught a usage mistake).

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.GpuOutOfMemory
member.summary: ET-P-0201 — wgpu out-of-memory error (allocation exceeded device limits).

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.GpuInternal
member.summary: ET-P-0202 — wgpu internal error (bug in wgpu-native or driver).

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.GpuUnknown
member.summary: ET-P-0203 — unknown wgpu error type.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.GpuReserved
member.summary: ET-P-0204 — reserved for future GPU error codes.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.SurfaceOutlivesInstance
member.summary: ET-P-0205 — surface outlives the instance it was created from.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.SurfaceHandleNull
member.summary: ET-P-0206 — a surface handle passed to the native layer was null.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.InvalidSwapChainSize
member.summary: ET-P-0207 — swap chain configured with zero or invalid dimensions.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.LabelTooLong
member.summary: ET-P-0208 — a debug label exceeds the maximum 127-byte length.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.TimestampQueryUnsupported
member.summary: ET-P-0209 — timestamp queries are not supported by the current adapter.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.GpuAdapterUnavailable
member.summary: ET-P-0210 — no GPU adapter available for off-screen rendering.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.GpuDeviceCreationFailed
member.summary: ET-P-0211 — wgpu device creation failed.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.GpuBufferMapTimeout
member.summary: ET-P-0212 — GPU buffer map timed out during read-back.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.DegenerateVector
member.summary: ET-P-0301 — a geometric operation received a degenerate input (e.g. normalising a zero-length vector).

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.NonInvertibleAffine
member.summary: ET-P-0302 — an affine transform is singular and cannot be inverted.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.InvertedRect
member.summary: ET-P-0303 — a Rect was constructed with MaxX < MinX or MaxY < MinY.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.InvalidCircle
member.summary: ET-P-0304 — a Circle was constructed with a negative radius.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.PathVerbWithoutMoveTo
member.summary: ET-P-0305 — a path verb that requires a prior MoveTo (e.g. LineTo) was emitted before any MoveTo.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.BuilderConsumed
member.summary: ET-P-0306 — Build() was called more than once on the same BezPathBuilder.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.SpanLengthMismatch
member.summary: ET-P-0307 — src/dst spans passed to a batch operation had mismatched lengths.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.FlattenSinkOverflow
member.summary: ET-P-0308 — a FlattenSink accepted a point when its buffer was already full and autoflush was disabled.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.UnsortedLengths
member.summary: ET-P-0309 — lengths passed to SampleAtLengthsSorted were not sorted in ascending order.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.SceneBuilderConsumed
member.summary: ET-P-0402 — SceneBuilder was used after End() was called.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.InvalidSceneResourceId
member.summary: ET-P-0403 — an invalid resource ID was passed to a SceneBuilder draw method.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.SceneFormatBadMagic
member.summary: ET-P-0404 — scene format buffer does not start with the ETSC magic bytes.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.SceneFormatVersionTooNew
member.summary: ET-P-0405 — scene format major version is newer than supported.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.SceneFormatTruncated
member.summary: ET-P-0406 — scene format buffer is too short to contain the declared header.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.BadFrameMarkers
member.summary: ET-P-0420 — scene frame markers are malformed (duplicate/missing BeginFrame/EndFrame).

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.UnbalancedLayerStack
member.summary: ET-P-0421 — PopLayer without a matching PushLayer.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.LayerStackOverflow
member.summary: ET-P-0422 — PushLayer depth exceeds 32.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.UnbalancedClipStack
member.summary: ET-P-0423 — PopClip without a matching PushClip.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.InvalidResourceId
member.summary: ET-P-0424 — a scene command references a resource ID that does not exist.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.NonFiniteGeometry
member.summary: ET-P-0425 — NaN or Infinity found in transform or geometry coordinates.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.EmptyPath
member.summary: ET-P-0426 — a path used in FillPath or StrokePath has fewer than 2 verbs.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.BadGradient
member.summary: ET-P-0427 — a gradient paint has fewer than 2 stops or non-monotone offsets.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.BadLayerOpacity
member.summary: ET-P-0428 — PushLayer opacity is outside the valid range [0, 1].

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.BadStrokeParam
member.summary: ET-P-0429 — stroke width is not > 0, or miter limit is < 1.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.InvalidSurfaceSize
member.summary: ET-P-0501 — surface or grid dimensions are <= 0.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.AccumulatorConsumed
member.summary: ET-P-0502 — ClassificationAccumulator.Finish() called more than once.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.SchedulerRequired
member.summary: ET-P-0503 — a null ITileScheduler was passed to ParallelClassifier.Classify.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.ShaderCompileError
member.summary: ET-P-0601 — a shader failed to compile at runtime (WebGPU validation error).

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.ClipStackOverflow
member.summary: ET-P-0702 — PushClip depth exceeds 16 in CPU rasterizer.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.StripBudgetExceeded
member.summary: ET-P-0801 — strip + coverage arena exceeds 64 MiB budget per frame.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.UnsortedDrawOrder
member.summary: ET-P-0802 — draw order is not sorted by (layer, commandIndex) in DEBUG mode.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.GpuClipStackOverflow
member.summary: ET-P-0803 — GPU clip mask stack exceeds 16 levels.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.ClipStackTooDeep
member.summary: ET-P-0902 — PushClip depth exceeds 16.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.DegenerateRadialGradient
member.summary: ET-P-0903 — a radial gradient was created with a zero or negative radius.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.DegenerateSweepGradient
member.summary: ET-P-0904 — a sweep gradient was created with endAngle <= startAngle.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.DegenerateDashPattern
member.summary: ET-P-1001 — a dash pattern was created with null/empty segments or zero sum.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.InvalidAtlasDimension
member.summary: ET-P-1101 — a glyph atlas dimension was not 2048 or 4096.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.AtlasInsertFailedAfterEviction
member.summary: ET-P-1102 — eviction freed space but insert still failed.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.IcuDataMissing
member.summary: ET-P-1103 — ICU4N data files are missing or could not be loaded.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.ImageDecodeFailed
member.summary: ET-P-1201 — SharpImage failed to decode the image data.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.ImageDimensionsExceedLimit
member.summary: ET-P-1202 — decoded image dimensions exceed the device's maxTextureDimension2D limit.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.DegenerateMesh
member.summary: ET-P-1601 — a mesh gradient was created with fewer than 2 rows or 2 columns, or vertex count doesn't match.

member.name: F:Etch.PanicCodes.DegenerateNoise
member.summary: ET-P-1602 — noise spec created with invalid octaves, negative scale, or zero opacity.

member.name: T:Etch.EtchLogAttribute
member.summary: Marks a static partial method as a structured-logging entry point. The EtchLoggerGenerator source-generator emits a zero-allocation implementation that checks IsEnabled, builds a stackalloc key/value span, and forwards to IEtchLogger.Log.
member.remarks: The attributed method must be static partial, take IEtchLogger as its first parameter, and take additional parameters matching the placeholder names in Template by index position. Placeholders use the {name} convention matching .NET string interpolation but without the $ prefix.

member.name: P:Etch.EtchLogAttribute.EventId
member.summary: Application-defined event identifier passed through to IEtchLogger.Log.

member.name: P:Etch.EtchLogAttribute.Level
member.summary: The minimum EtchLogLevel at which this message should be emitted.

member.name: P:Etch.EtchLogAttribute.Template
member.summary: Message template with named placeholders, e.g. Tile {tileIndex} classified with {stripCount} strips. Placeholders map to method parameters by zero-based index position (excluding the leading IEtchLogger parameter).

member.name: T:Etch.EtchLogLevel
member.summary: Log event severity levels. Errors at and above are routed through rather than — panic codes carry the machine-readable incident key that logs and crash dumps can correlate on.

member.name: T:Etch.IEtchLogger
member.summary: Minimal structured-logging sink for Etch hot paths. Consumers (e.g. CascadeUI) bridge this to their platform logger (ILogger, spdlog, etc.) in one line.

remarks.para: Design constraints. No scopes, no filters, no generics. The single method accepts a of key/value pairs so that generated call sites can use stackalloc and stay allocation-free on every call. Consumers who need filtering do it in their bridge layer before calling .
Panic vs. logging. and above are still routed through at the call site — IEtchLogger is for operational diagnostics only, not exception-shaped failures.

member.name: M:Etch.IEtchLogger.IsEnabled(Etch.EtchLogLevel)
member.summary: Returns if any message at would be emitted.

member.name: M:Etch.IEtchLogger.Log(Etch.EtchLogLevel,System.Int32,System.String,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.Object}})
member.summary: Emits a structured log event. The is the message with named placeholders, e.g. Tile {tileIndex} classified. The span provides the argument values in matching index order. Callers generated by EtchLoggerGenerator use stackalloc to avoid heap allocation.
member.param: Argument values, one per placeholder in .

member.name: T:Etch.NullEtchLogger
member.summary: Default no-op logger. always returns and does nothing. AOT-clean, zero allocation, safe to use as a compilation-unit-level singleton where no logging backend is wired up yet.

member.name: P:Etch.NullEtchLogger.Instance
member.summary: Shared singleton instance. Safe to use wherever a is required but no logging backend has been configured yet.
